content: Hey, I'm trying to organize my climbing gear for an upcoming expedition. I've got three different routes planned - one in Patagonia, one in the Dolomites, and a local one in Yosemite. Need to make sure I've got everything sorted.
That sounds like an exciting trip! Are you climbing solo or with a team? And what time of year are you heading out?

I'm going with my usual partner, Sam Chen. We've been climbing together for about 8 years now. The Patagonia trip is in December, Dolomites in July, and Yosemite in September. I'm also leading a group of beginners in Yosemite, so I need to pack extra gear for that.
Eight years with Sam is impressive! What kind of extra gear do you need for the beginner group? Are you teaching specific techniques?

Yeah, we're doing a three-day intro course. I need to bring extra harnesses, helmets, and those beginner-friendly ATC devices. I'm also packing my origami paper - I like to teach them how to fold cranes during breaks. It's surprisingly calming after a tough climb.
Origami on climbing trips? That's unique! Do you find it helps with focus or just relaxation? I'd love to hear more about your teaching approach.

Both, actually. The folding helps with finger dexterity too. I start with simple folds and work up to more complex ones. Last month I taught a group in Joshua Tree and they loved it. I'm also preparing for my move to Taiwan next spring - been taking Spanish classes to prepare, though I know that's not the right language for Taiwan!
Haha, Spanish classes for Taiwan is quite the mix-up! What prompted the move? And are you planning to continue guiding there or try something new?

My partner got a job at a tech company in Taipei. I'm planning to transition into urban sketching while I'm there - there's so much architecture to capture. I've been practicing by sketching our local climbing gym. Also, I'm working on some new comedy material about climbing mishaps for an open mic night next week.
Urban sketching in Taipei sounds amazing! And comedy about climbing mishaps? I'd love to hear one of those stories. What's been your funniest climbing moment so far?

Oh man, last year in Red Rocks I accidentally wore my harness backwards for the first two pitches. Sam didn't notice until I couldn't figure out how to clip in properly. We laughed about it for hours. I'm also thinking of starting a YouTube channel about beginner climbing tips - maybe call it 'Kit's Climbing Corner' or something.

Wearing the harness backwards is hilarious! 'Kit's Climbing Corner' has a nice ring to it. What kind of content were you thinking of starting with? Maybe that harness story could be your first blooper reel!

Exactly! I was thinking of starting with gear basics, common mistakes, and then some of those calming origami tutorials. I've already got my first 10 video ideas mapped out. Gotta run though - I have a Spanish class in 20 minutes and I'm still trying to figure out why I'm learning it for Taiwan!
